amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Two-component systems in E. coli sense environmental signals via sensor histidine kinases that autophosphorylate and transfer phosphate to response regulators. The EnvZ-OmpR system exemplifies this: EnvZ senses osmolarity, autophosphorylates His243, transfers phosphate to OmpR Asp55, and OmpR~P regulates porin genes (OmpF/OmpC). E. coli has ~30 two-component systems controlling diverse responses. The basic mechanism: signal → kinase autophosphorylation → phosphotransfer → transcriptional response.

name: Two-Component Signal Transduction (EnvZ-OmpR Paradigm)
notes: Paradigm for bacterial signal transduction. E. coli has ~30 TCS systems. Shows AND gates (4) for phosphotransfer requirements.
scientificAccuracy: Based on comprehensive characterization of E. coli two-component systems.

sources.title: Two-component signal transduction.
sources.authors: Stock, AM., Robinson, VL., Goudreau, PN.

sources.title: Two genes, ompR and envZ, specify the two regulatory components of the major outer membrane porin proteins of Escherichia coli K-12.
Structure and Mechanism of Two-Component Signal Transduction Systems.
